reasoning: The Lean statement asserts `generateFrom A = generateFrom (sInter {T | is_topology X T ∧ A ⊆ T})`. The right-hand side inner expression is the intersection of all topologies (as sets of sets) containing A, which is itself a topology; applying `generateFrom` to a set that's already a topology recovers that topology. So the RHS as a `TopologicalSpace` corresponds exactly to "the topology whose opens are the intersection of all topologies containing A" — matching the informal RHS. The LHS `generateFrom A` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "topology generated by A". The `is_topology` predicate correctly encodes the topology axioms (∅ ∈ T follows from sUnion ∅). The "if A is a subbasis" phrasing in the informal statement is standard setup language; the conclusion holds for any collection A, so omitting an explicit subbasis/coverage hypothesis is not a meaningful change. The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
